title: cistus vulgaris var. villosus
intro: Cistus vulgaris var. villosus, also known as the hairy or villosus rock rose, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its pinkish-purple flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cistus vulgaris var. villosus include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
